extracted.brandBackground: ComForCare offers franchises for non-medical personal care, companionship, and on-site community care, with optional private duty nursing services.

leadership.name: J.J. Sorrenti
leadership.background: CEO of parent Best Life Brands, LLC since March 2020.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides senior executive leadership across the parent brand portfolio.

leadership.name: Kevin Vesely
leadership.background: CFO of Best Life Brands, LLC since September 2023; former VP of Finance for Sonova, Inc.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs financial strategy and controls for the franchise system.

leadership.name: Jennifer LoBianco
leadership.background: CMO since December 2022; former Senior VP of Marketing.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads national brand marketing and customer acquisition strategies.

leadership.name: Rebecca Bouchard

leadership.whyItMatters: Directly oversees ComForCare brand operations and franchisee support.

leadership.name: David Tarr
leadership.background: VP of Franchise Development since November 2022.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages franchise system expansion and territory sales.

leadership.name: Stephen D. Greenwald
leadership.background: In-house counsel since inception and general counsel of parent Best Life Brands, LLC.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages legal compliance and franchise agreement enforcement.

cohorts.label: Table A - Gross Sales Information by Franchise Territory Operating at Least One Full Year
cohorts.basis: Average gross sales of 220 franchised territories open and operating for the entire 2025 measurement period.

cohorts.label: Table B - Gross Sales Information by Franchise Owner Operating at Least One Full Year
cohorts.basis: Average gross sales of 154 franchise owners operating for at least one full year, aggregating multi-territory owners.

item19.notes: Table A excludes 30 territories open less than a year, 3 territories that failed to report, and 7 undeveloped/pending territories.

lineItems.notes: Standard single unit initial franchise fee.
Cost of travel and lodging for training.
Estimated rent and deposits for 3 months.
Hardware, software, and setup fees for 3 months.
Utilities, business licenses, and legal fees.
Initial deposit for required insurance policies.
First 3 months of local marketing spend.

hiddenCosts.name: essentialALZ Exam Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Certification exam fee per person, renewed bi-annually.

hiddenCosts.name: Telehealth Fees
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory participation in approved Telehealth programs.

hiddenCosts.name: Annual Conference Absentee Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Penalty fee charged if the franchisee fails to attend the mandatory Annual Conference.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: The franchise agreement requires mediation, arbitration, and litigation to take place only in Michigan.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee spouses must sign a personal guarantee making them personally liable for all financial obligations.

operationalRisks.title: Sales Performance Required
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must maintain minimum billable hour levels or face territory reduction or termination.

extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise provides non-medical home care, companionship, and wellness services to seniors and individuals aging in place.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires a full-time managing owner, at least one administrative/marketing employee, a caregiver recruiter, and a part-time nurse.

riskReasons: 2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.

midCohort.label: Table A - Gross Sales Information by Franchise Territory Operating at Least One Full Year

source.label: Table A - Gross Sales Information by Franchise Territory Operating at Least One Full Year

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $133,200 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $105,803/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise provides non-medical home care, companionship, and wellness services to seniors and individuals aging in place.

benchmark.label: Health & wellness (med-spa, IV, longevity, recovery)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Licensed clinical labor plus regulatory/insurance load and equipment depreciation. These concepts frequently disclose NO Item 19, so benchmarks matter most precisely where extraction gives you the least.
considerations: Product/consumables run ~15–25%; clinical/licensed labor is expensive at ~25–35%.
Factor heavy equipment depreciation, malpractice/liability insurance, and regulatory compliance — none of it is in the FDD.
With little or no Item 19, validate revenue per treatment room and ramp directly with Item 20 franchisees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 12–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 7.8–10.9 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Health & wellness (med-spa, IV, longevity, recovery)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken from the FDD (~$1,500/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 15–25% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 25–35% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing; the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. CFC Holding Company, LLC guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
